ideas.title: Window Sky Hours 20-23: dusk Label on a Night Gradient With No Stars
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 811: const timeLabel uses a final else that labels hours 20-23 as dusk. But getGradient(20) returns linear-gradient(to bottom, #4c1d95, #312e81) — deep purple night. And isMidnight is hour >= 0 && hour <= 4 only, so hours 20-23 get zero stars. The window says dusk while looking like the void of space. Fix: rename isMidnight to isDeepNight and extend it to hour >= 20 || hour <= 4. Rename the timeLabel for hours 20-23 from dusk to night. Two small changes. This has appeared in every review since 75. Six reviews. It must end here.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Wraps to 1/9 But Shows Bonus[0] — Content and Counter Out of Sync
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1396: counter is (quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1. With 8 bonus taglines denominator is 9. When quoteBonus reaches 9 (after clicking through all bonuses): counter shows (9 % 9) + 1 = 1, so 1/9. But content is BONUS_TAGLINES[(9-1) % 8] = BONUS_TAGLINES[0] — the first bonus which is quote 2 of 9 total. Counter says 1, content is 2. Out of sync. Fix: derive the counter from the content position. The shown quote index is (quoteBonus - 1) % 8 for bonus quotes. Its position in the full 9-quote sequence is ((quoteBonus - 1) % 8) + 2 (since vibe tagline is 1). Use that as numerator.

ideas.title: idleBubble Emoji Content Index Still Rides the Global Wall Clock
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 747: content index is Math.floor((Date.now() + (worker.id?.charCodeAt(0) ?? 0) * 4000) / 12000) % length. The bubble TIMER is now per-worker via wanderIdx stagger, which is good. But the content selection is still a global 12-second wall clock with per-worker offsets. At any given second you can predict every worker emoji from the formula. Fix: inside the idle micro-bubble useEffect, store a random content index in a useRef and advance it randomly when the bubble shows, completely decoupling content from wall time.

ideas.title: ProgressBar updatedAt Prop Is Declared But Never Referenced Inside the Component
ideas.description: App.jsx around line 198: function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt }). The updatedAt parameter is destructured but never used in the component body. The ETA calculation uses startedAt only. The formatDuration call that uses updatedAt happens in WorkerCard, not ProgressBar. The prop exists in the contract but is honored by no one. Either remove it from the signature, or use it as a fallback when startedAt is absent: const workingMs = (startedAt || updatedAt) ? Date.now() - new Date(startedAt || updatedAt).getTime() : null. This has been open since review 75.

ideas.title: wanderIdx Caps at 4 — With Five Idle Workers Two Share the Same Bubble Interval
ideas.description: Office.jsx idle workers render: const wIdx = (i % 4) + 1. Worker index 0 and worker index 4 both get wIdx = 1. Their idle bubble timer is 8000 + 1 * 3000 = 11000ms. Both fire at the same moment. With all five workers idle (Carlos Maya Dave Sofia Luna), two will always synchronize their bubble display defeating the stagger intent. Fix: use a hash of the worker ID for a stable but diverse offset instead of wanderIdx, or simply let i run freely without the modulo cap since there are only ever 5 workers.

ideas.title: WorkerCard Outer Div Has tabIndex={0} With No Interaction Handlers
ideas.description: App.jsx line 302: the outer div has tabIndex={0} and role=article making it a keyboard tab stop. But there is no onKeyDown, no onClick, nothing. Keyboard users tab to the card and pressing Enter or Space does nothing. The inner info button handles the flip correctly, but the card itself is a dead focus stop. Fix: add onKeyDown to the outer div that calls setIsFlipped(true) on Enter or Space, matching what the info button does. Or remove tabIndex={0} and expose the card exclusively through its button children. The current state is a silent accessibility failure.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Missing Refactor Category — Engineering Invisible Work
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 165-177: TASK_TAG_DEFS covers Bug Ship Design QA Data Docs Plan. No pattern for refactor or performance work. Tasks like refactor auth layer, performance optimization, clean up tech debt produce no tag. Engineering's most honest work is invisible to the dashboard. Fix: add pattern /refactor|cleanup|clean.?up|tech.?debt|optimize|performance|perf/i with label Refactor, emoji wrench, className task-tag--refactor, and a distinct CSS color like #14b8a6 teal.

ideas.title: Character Non-Manager pingReaction Sets bubble State That Is Never Displayed
ideas.description: Office.jsx lines 533-537: the pingReaction useEffect runs for ALL Character variants and calls setBubble({ quote: pingReaction, show: true }). But for non-manager workers the speech bubble renders from the pingReaction prop directly at line 638, not from bubble.quote. The setBubble call for non-managers triggers a state update and re-render whose result is never read. The setTimeout to reset bubble.show also fires 3 seconds later for another orphan re-render. Fix: guard the setBubble and timerRef block with an early return if variant !== manager.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Ref Missing startedAt — Elapsed Meeting Time Is Architecturally Blocked
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1172: const lastHuddle = useRef({ names: [], endedAt: null }). When a huddle starts at line 1173: lastHuddle.current = { names: meetingWorkers.map(w => w.name), endedAt: null }. No startedAt is ever set. ConferenceTable cannot show elapsed time during an active huddle because start time was never captured. Fix: add startedAt: Date.now() when setting the active huddle. ConferenceTable can then compute Date.now() - lastHuddle.current.startedAt to render a live meeting timer like In huddle for 8m. Flagged in review 76.

ideas.title: Ghost OOO Bubble Knows the Role But Not the Last Deed
ideas.description: Office.jsx ghost variant around line 702: the OOO bubble shows role and last-seen time. But activityEntries is already passed in and lastEvent is already fetched for relTime. The message field is right there. If the last activity was Shipped auth migration the ghost bubble could whisper: Last shipped: Shipped auth migration. Each ghost gets a eulogy instead of just a timestamp. One extra line: render lastEvent?.message to give absent workers a story rather than a vanishing act.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Doodles Are Static — They Should Escalate With vibeStreak
ideas.description: Office.jsx around line 1313: five static ASCII faces, one per vibe, never change. But Office already receives vibeStreak as a prop. At streak 1 the crushing face is calm. At streak 5 it gets excited. At streak 10 legendary tier it should be full chaos energy. At streak 1 on-fire is tense. At streak 5 it escalates. Let the whiteboard doodle breathe with the room. It is the emotional thermometer of the office floor and right now it has three settings: fine, fine, and fine.

theme: Puppeteer timed out — the code review didn't — zero fixes since #79, two new timer traps, and the dusk window has been wrong for eight reviews
visual_observations: Screenshot unavailable: Puppeteer launch timed out in this environment; full code review conducted instead against App.jsx (1402 lines), Office.jsx (1597 lines), Office.css (2406 lines), App.css (1164 lines)
plantToast (Office.jsx line 1238) and completionToast (App.jsx line 1126) both render at position: fixed, bottom: 2rem, left: 50%, zIndex: 9999 — they occupy the literal same pixel

WindowElement (Office.jsx line 807): timeLabel shows 'dusk' for hours 20-23 via `hour <= 19 ? 'golden hour' : 'dusk'`, but getGradient returns deep purple `#4c1d95 → #312e81` for those hours, and isMidnight check (0-4) means hours 20-23 get zero stars — night sky, dusk label, empty

TASK_TAG_DEFS (App.jsx line 169): pattern `/test|qa|spec|review|coverage|lint/i` — the bare word 'review' matches any task containing 'Design review', 'Sprint review', 'Architecture review', 'Creative review', stamping a 🔵 QA badge on planning and design work

justShipped.forEach (App.jsx lines 956-965): outer `setTimeout(() => {...}, i * 2600)` IDs are discarded — the `return` values of these setTimeout calls are never stored in any ref. Only the two inner refs (workerConfettiTimerRef, completionToastTimerRef) are tracked, and those refs get silently overwritten on each iteration of the loop

ActivityLog component (App.jsx line 135) caps visible entries at `.slice(0, 20)` but the header (line 1325) renders `${activityLog.length} events` against the full array — if 50 events exist the header reads '50 events' while 30 are invisible

Tab title effect (App.jsx line 1054): `let idx = 0; document.title = frames[idx]` executes on every change to [activeWorkers, teamVibe] — every 30s poll resets to frame 0. Frame 1 (worker breakdown) and Frame 2 (vibe label) are only visible if no poll fires for 8–16 seconds after the effect runs

WallClock commentary (Office.jsx line 1049): the five vibe-specific quotes live in a `<title>` element inside a 22×22px SVG. Title tooltips are hover-only on desktop and completely inaccessible on touch devices — this is the entire deliverable of the WallClock vibe feature

PING_REACTIONS (Office.jsx line 163): exactly one static string per worker — `luna: 'Creative review in progress...'`, `carlos: 'Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.'`, etc. Click any worker once or a hundred times: same string, same punctuation, same energy

ProgressBar component signature (App.jsx line 195): `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })` — `updatedAt` is declared and passed by the caller at line 350 but never referenced anywhere in the function body; only `startedAt` is used for ETA calculation

DOOR_LABELS (Office.jsx line 139) has five keys with emoji-prefixed copy (🚀, 🧯, ⚡, ☁️, 🔒) but the door render (line 1468) ignores those embedded icons entirely: `vibe === 'after-hours' ? '🔒' : doorEvent === 'arrive' ? '🚪✨' : '🚪'` — three states for five vibes, the DOOR_LABELS emoji is visible only in the label text below

Ficus growth toast (Office.jsx line 1260) reads `🌱 The office ficus leveled up!` for all five stage transitions — same line whether the plant graduates from seedling to sprout or from leafy to blooming

ideas.title: plantToast and completionToast Render at Identical Fixed Coordinates — One Erases the Other
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1238 and App.jsx line 1126 both use `position: fixed, bottom: 2rem, left: 50%, transform: translateX(-50%), zIndex: 9999`. When a worker ships a task AND the ficus levels up in the same vibe window (both plausible on a 'crushing' day), completionToast overwrites plantToast visually at the same pixel. Fix: stagger the vertical position — plant at `bottom: 5.5rem`, completion at `bottom: 2rem` — or introduce a toast queue that displays them sequentially.

ideas.title: justShipped.forEach Fires Outer setTimeout with Untracked IDs — Multi-Ship Leaves Zombie Timers
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 956-965: `justShipped.forEach((worker, i) => { setTimeout(() => { setWorkerConfetti(...); setCompletionToast(...); workerConfettiTimerRef.current = setTimeout(...) ... }, i * 2600) })`. The outer `setTimeout` return values are discarded — no ref holds their IDs. If two workers ship simultaneously and a 30s poll fires within 2.6s, the effect re-runs, but the pending outer timer for worker[0] at `+0ms` and worker[1] at `+2600ms` cannot be cancelled. They fire into stale state. Additionally, `workerConfettiTimerRef.current` and `completionToastTimerRef.current` are overwritten on each forEach iteration — only the last worker's inner cleanup is tracked. Store outer IDs in a ref array and clear them on effect cleanup.

ideas.title: Activity Log Header Reports Full Array Length While Component Caps Render at 20 — Open Since Review #74
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1325: header renders `${activityLog.length} events` (or filtered count). ActivityLog component line 135: `[...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20)` — hard cap at 20 displayed. With 50 events in the log the header says '50 events' and 30 are invisible with no scroll, no pagination, no indication. Either raise the cap to match what's reported, add pagination, or change the header to say 'showing 20 of N events' so the user knows there's more.

ideas.title: Window Sky Hours 20-23 Renders Night Gradient With Zero Stars Under a 'Dusk' Label — Eight Reviews
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 807: `hour <= 19 ? 'golden hour' : 'dusk'` — hours 20-23 get the label 'dusk'. Line 800: `getGradient` for hours 20-23 returns `linear-gradient(to bottom, #4c1d95, #312e81)` — that is astronomical night, not dusk. Line 815: `isMidnight = hour >= 0 && hour <= 4` — hours 20-23 get no stars at all. Fix: rename label to 'night' for hours 20-23, add stars via the same `office-window__star` spans used for midnight, or extend midnight hours definition to cover 20-4.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS 'review' in the QA Pattern Stamps a Blue QA Badge on Design Reviews, Sprint Reviews, and Creative Reviews
ideas.description: App.jsx line 169: `/test|qa|spec|review|coverage|lint/i` — the bare word 'review' is semantically overloaded. A task like 'Design review with stakeholders' matches both the Design pattern (🟣) and this QA pattern (🔵), mislabeling a design ceremony as a QA activity. 'Sprint review' matches both Plan and QA. 'Creative review in progress' matches QA. Change the pattern to `code.?review|peer.?review|qa|spec|test|coverage|lint` to require the word 'review' to be qualified by 'code' or 'peer' before triggering the QA tag.

ideas.title: Tab Title idx Resets to 0 on Every 30s Poll — Frames 1 and 2 Never Display in Normal Usage
ideas.description: App.jsx lines 1054-1057: the title effect depends on `[activeWorkers, teamVibe]`, which changes every poll. On each run: `let idx = 0; document.title = frames[idx]; setInterval(...)`. Every 30s the effect re-runs, sets idx=0, clears the old interval, and starts a new one. Frame 0 is permanently shown; frames 1 and 2 (worker breakdown and vibe label) require 8s and 16s of no-poll to appear — never in practice. Fix: move `idx` into a `useRef` so it persists across effect re-runs triggered by count changes, only resetting when the frame count changes.

ideas.title: WallClock Vibe Commentary Exists Only Inside a 22px SVG title — Hover-Only, Touch-Never
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1049: `<title>{displayH}:{displayM} {AM/PM} — {CLOCK_COMMENTS[vibeKey]}</title>` inside a 22×22px SVG. Browser tooltip on hover (desktop only). Five beautifully written vibe-specific clock comments — 'You have checked this three times in the last hour', 'Time has no meaning during an incident' — are inaccessible on every mobile device and invisible to keyboard/screen reader users. Surface them as an `aria-describedby` tooltip component, or render the comment as a visible label that appears on focus/hover with a proper tooltip role.

ideas.title: Door Icon Has Three States for Five Vibes — DOOR_LABELS Emoji Is Unreachable From the Door Render
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1468: door icon logic is `vibe === 'after-hours' ? '🔒' : doorEvent === 'arrive' ? '🚪✨' : '🚪'`. Three branches cover all five vibes. DOOR_LABELS (line 139) contains `'crushing': '🚀 All hands in'`, `'on-fire': '🧯 Brace yourself'` etc., but those emoji are rendered only in the label text below the icon, not in the icon itself. Add a DOOR_ICONS map keyed by vibe (`crushing: '🚪💨'`, `on-fire: '🚨'`, `in-flow: '🚪'`, `slow-day: '☁️🚪'`, `after-hours: '🔒'`) so the icon communicates the vibe, not just the last entry event.

ideas.title: ProgressBar updatedAt Prop Is Declared, Passed, and Silently Ignored — Dead Contract in the Signature
ideas.description: App.jsx line 195: `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })`. The caller at line 350 passes `updatedAt={worker.updated_at}`. Inside the component, only `startedAt` is used (line 216 for ETA). `updatedAt` is never read. Either remove it from the signature and call site to clean the contract, or use it — `updatedAt` would enable a 'stalled progress' indicator when `updatedAt` is old but `progress < 100`, surfacing workers whose tasks haven't moved in hours.

ideas.title: Ficus Growth Toast Is One Line for All Five Plant Life Events — Seedling to Blooming Gets the Same Eulogy
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1260: `🌱 The office ficus leveled up!` fires for every stage transition regardless of which stage was reached. A plant crossing from seedling to sprout is a very different moment from crossing from leafy to blooming. Define a PLANT_GROWTH_MESSAGES map: `{ sprout: '🌱 First signs of life in the corner', 'small-plant': '🌿 The ficus is finding its confidence', leafy: '🌿 Full foliage achieved', blooming: '🌸 Peak ficus. The office smells great.' }` and index it by `plantStage` when the toast fires.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS Has Exactly One Static String Per Worker — Click Someone 100 Times, Same Line Every Time
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 163: `PING_REACTIONS` is a flat object with one string per worker ID. `luna: 'Creative review in progress...'` — always. `carlos: 'Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.'` — always. At the most interactive moment in the UI (clicking a character), the response is deterministic and immediately stale on the second click. Replace each value with an array of 3-5 reactions, pick with `Math.floor(Math.random() * arr.length)`, and make them context-aware: `carlos` at after-hours should say something different than `carlos` at crushing. This is the cheapest character depth investment in the codebase.

ideas.title: completionToast Says 'shipped it!' for Every Worker — Zero Character Voice at Peak Emotional Moment
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1148: `🎉 ${completionToast.name} shipped it!` — one sentence covers all five workers across all five vibes. Maya ships: '🎉 Maya shipped it!'. Carlos ships at 11pm: '🎉 Carlos shipped it!'. This is the highest-emotion moment in the UI and it sounds like a CI system. Define per-worker completion lines per vibe in WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES, e.g., `carlos.crushing: '📊 Carlos closed the ticket. The logs are clean.'`, `sofia['on-fire']: '🔍 Sofia found it, fixed it, and shipped it. Chaos briefly contained.'`. The toast is 3 seconds — make those 3 seconds count.

ideas.description: Office.jsx: five vibe-specific clock comments live inside an SVG title element on a 22x22px element. These are hover-only on desktop and completely inaccessible on touch devices and screen readers. 'You have checked this three times in the last hour' and 'Time has no meaning during an incident' are the best-written lines in the codebase and they are invisible to most users. Fix: surface the comment as a visible caption that appears on focus or hover with proper role tooltip.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Wraps to 1/9 at quoteBonus=9 While Displaying Bonus[0] Content — Counter and Slot Out of Phase
ideas.description: App.jsx footer: (quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1 with length=8 gives denominator 9. At quoteBonus=9 (first wrap): counter = (9%9)+1 = 1, but content is BONUS_TAGLINES[0] — the first bonus tagline, not the vibe tagline. Counter reads '1/9' while displaying slot-2 content. After one full cycle the counter and content are permanently phase-shifted. Fix: reset quoteBonus to 1 at wrap so the vibe tagline genuinely occupies slot 1 and bonuses occupy slots 2-9.

ideas.title: plantStageNames Array Declared Inside Office Component but Never Referenced — Dead Variable
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1107: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] — declared in the component body, referenced nowhere in the file. It is orphaned from an incomplete refactor, likely intended to anchor the per-stage Ficus toast messages that still have not been implemented. Remove it or wire it to a PLANT_GROWTH_MESSAGES map — two birds, one refactor. Dead declarations in hot-path component bodies waste attention on every code read.

ideas.title: Door Icon Has Three States for Five Vibes — DOOR_LABELS Emoji Is Stranded in the Caption
ideas.description: Office.jsx: the door icon renders: after-hours = locked, arrive event = door-sparkle, otherwise = door. Three branches for five vibes. DOOR_LABELS has rich per-vibe copy with emoji (All hands in, Brace yourself, Focus zone) but those emoji are rendered only as caption text below the icon, never in the icon itself. Add a DOOR_ICONS map keyed by vibe so the icon communicates the vibe even when no door event is in progress.

ideas.title: ProgressBar updatedAt Prop Declared, Passed, and Never Read — Dead Contract in the Signature
ideas.description: App.jsx: function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt }) — the caller passes updatedAt={worker.updated_at} but inside the component only startedAt is used for ETA. updatedAt is never referenced. Either remove it from the signature and call site to clean the contract, or use it: updatedAt would enable a stalled indicator when progress < 100 and updatedAt is older than 30 minutes, surfacing workers whose tasks have not moved without reaching the 2-hour stuck threshold in StatsBar.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Ref Still Missing startedAt — Elapsed Meeting Time Remains Architecturally Blocked
ideas.description: Office.jsx: when meetingWorkers.length >= 2, lastHuddle.current is set to { names: [...], endedAt: null }. No startedAt field. The ConferenceTable shows how long ago the last meeting ended but cannot show how long the current meeting has been running because the start time is never captured. Fix: capture startedAt only on the 0-to-2 worker transition (when endedAt !== null OR names is empty), then display elapsed duration on the Huddle badge during live meetings.

ideas.title: Ficus Growth Toast Is One Generic Line for All Five Stage Transitions — Same Eulogy from Seedling to Blooming
ideas.description: Office.jsx: plantToast always renders 'The office ficus leveled up!' regardless of which stage was reached. Seedling to sprout and leafy to blooming are very different moments. Define a PLANT_GROWTH_MESSAGES map: { sprout: 'First signs of life in the corner', 'small-plant': 'The ficus is finding its confidence', leafy: 'Full foliage achieved', blooming: 'Peak ficus. The office smells incredible.' }. The declared but unused plantStageNames variable is the perfect anchor for this map — two birds, one refactor.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS Has One Static String Per Worker — Click Someone 100 Times, Same Sentence Every Time
ideas.description: Office.jsx: PING_REACTIONS is a flat object with one string per worker ID. 'luna: Creative review in progress...' Always. The click handler does const reaction = PING_REACTIONS[worker.id] — no array, no randomness, no vibe context. At the most interactive moment in the UI the response is deterministic and stale after the second click. Replace each value with an array of 4-6 reactions, pick with Math.random(), and optionally vary by vibe: carlos at after-hours says something different than carlos at crushing. Cheapest character depth investment in the codebase.

ideas.title: completionToast Says 'shipped it!' for Every Worker — Zero Character Voice at the Peak Emotional Moment
ideas.description: App.jsx: the toast renders 'worker name + shipped it!' — one sentence for five workers across five vibes. The toast is 3 seconds at maximum visibility and it sounds like a CI webhook. Define WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES keyed by worker id and optionally vibe: carlos.crushing = 'Carlos closed the ticket. Logs are clean.', sofia.default = 'Sofia shipped it. Found two more bugs on the way.', maya['in-flow'] = 'Every pixel exactly right. Maya shipped it.' Three seconds of undivided user attention — make them count.

screenshot_note: Puppeteer failed: ProtocolError — Network.enable timed out on launch (same container-level network isolation as reviews 78–81). Full code audit conducted: App.jsx (1416 lines), Office.jsx (1598 lines), App.css (1164 lines), Office.css (2406 lines), utils/constants.js, utils/quotes.js, utils/vibe.js.
theme: The fix was always half-there — ghost refs, double emojis, and a tooltip that changes personality depending on who else is loitering
visual_observations: App.jsx line 704: `const titleIdxRef = useRef(0) // persists tab-title rotation across effect re-runs` — the ref is declared, commented with exact intent, and never referenced anywhere in the file. The title effect at line 1064 still uses `let idx = 0`. The scaffolding for the fix exists. The wire was never plugged in.

Office.jsx line 1567–1572: the vibe-weather div renders `{VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS[vibe]}` followed by `{VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS[vibe]}`. VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS begins every value with the same emoji as VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS (e.g., '☀️ Clear skies...' / '☀️'). Visually: double emoji every vibe, always.

Office.jsx lines 1501–1516: `const WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS = { carlos: [...], maya: [...], ... }` is declared as a local const INSIDE the `idleWorkers.map()` callback. This object is freshly allocated on every render pass per idle worker. With 5 idle workers this is 5 allocations of the same 5-key object every render.

App.jsx line 389: `const VIBE_RANK = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` at module scope. App.jsx line 1002: `const vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` inside a useEffect body. Same 5-key object, defined twice, used in adjacent logic.

Office.jsx line 1511: idle wander tooltip is selected via `workerTooltips[i % workerTooltips.length]` where `i` is the loop index in `idleWorkers.map()`. Carlos alone gets tooltip[0]. Carlos with Maya both idle gets tooltip[1] (Carlos is still index 0, Maya is 1 — fine). But if Carlos is idle second (after Dave), he becomes index 1 and gets tooltip[1]. A worker's wandering thought changes based on who else chose to take a break, not on who Carlos is.
App.jsx line 195: `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })` — `updatedAt` destructured, passed at line 350, never referenced inside the function body. Nine-plus reviews. Still there.

App.jsx line 1410: footer quote counter formula `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1` — at quoteBonus=9 (first wrap after all 8 bonuses): `(9 % 9) + 1 = 1`, shows '1/9'. Content is `BONUS_TAGLINES[(9-1) % 8] = BONUS_TAGLINES[0]` — first bonus tagline, not the vibe tagline. Counter and content are phase-shifted by one slot after the first full cycle. Three reviews.

App.jsx line 704: `titleIdxRef` — `useRef(0)` — never passed to a dependency array, never read in the title effect, never written. A ghost variable with a comment that describes exactly what it was supposed to do.

Office.jsx line 1172–1177: `lastHuddle.current = { names: meetingWorkers.map(w => w.name), endedAt: null }` — no `startedAt`. The elapsed meeting time feature is architecturally blocked by a missing 10-character field. Third review.

Office.jsx line 1261: `🌱 The office ficus leveled up!` — same toast for all five stage transitions. `plantStageNames` array (line 1107) and `plantStage` string (line 1108) are both available at that scope. The data is there. The differentiation is not.

fixed_since_last_review.title: justShipped.forEach Outer setTimeout IDs Now Tracked — Zombie Timer Bug Closed
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx lines 957–958: `justShippedTimersRef.current.forEach(t => clearTimeout(t)); justShippedTimersRef.current = []` runs before the forEach. Line 971: `justShippedTimersRef.current.push(t)` stores each outer ID. Lines 976–979: the effect cleanup calls forEach clearTimeout again. All three pieces in place. Multi-ship no longer leaves zombie timers.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Activity Log Header Now Shows 'Latest 20 of N' When Entries Exceed Cap
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx lines 1335–1340: `const DISPLAY_CAP = 20` is now referenced in the header count expression. When filtered.length > 20, header reads 'Latest 20 of N events' — matching the hard-cap slice in the ActivityLog component. The invisible-entries gap is labelled.

fixed_since_last_review.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS Now Character-Specific by worker.id
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx lines 1503–1516: `WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS` is a local object keyed by worker.id with 3 character-appropriate tooltips per worker. Carlos gets server-metrics copy; Luna gets horizon-scanning copy. The slot-machine tooltip era is over.

ideas.title: ProgressBar updatedAt: Nine Reviews, One Destructure, Zero References — Choose a Path
ideas.description: App.jsx line 195: `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })`. `updatedAt` is destructured, passed at WorkerCard line 350 as `updatedAt={worker.updated_at}`, and never referenced once inside the component body. Only `startedAt` drives ETA logic. This dead contract has been flagged in reviews 71, 72, 74, 75, 77, 78, 79, 80, and 81. Nine reviews. Two paths: (A) remove `updatedAt` from the destructure and call site — clean the contract in five keystrokes; (B) fulfill it — render a subtle 'stalled' indicator when `progress < 100` and `Date.now() - new Date(updatedAt).getTime() > 1_800_000` (30 minutes of no movement), surfacing stuck tasks before they cross the 2-hour StatsBar threshold. Path B converts nine reviews of dead code into a live staleness signal. WHY: nine reviews. This is not complexity. This is inertia. One decision and five minutes of work ends it.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Phase-Shifts After First Full Cycle — Three Reviews of Counter vs. Content Drift
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1410: `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1` with `BONUS_TAGLINES.length = 8` yields denominator 9. At quoteBonus=9 (after one complete cycle through all 8 bonus quotes): formula gives `(9 % 9) + 1 = 1`, displays '1/9'. But the content is `BONUS_TAGLINES[(9-1) % 8] = BONUS_TAGLINES[0]` — the first bonus tagline, occupying slot 2 in the original sequence. Counter says 1, content is 2. From this point forward the counter is permanently off by one from the content slot. Every subsequent wrap deepens the drift. Fix: when `quoteBonus > 0`, derive numerator from actual content position — `((quoteBonus - 1) % BONUS_TAGLINES.length) + 2` to preserve the vibe-tagline-as-slot-1 semantics. This has been flagged in reviews 79, 80, and 81 — escalated to must. WHY: a counter that lies about position isn't a UI decoration, it's a broken promise to every user who clicked past the default tagline.

ideas.title: titleIdxRef Was Declared to Fix Tab Title Rotation but Was Never Wired In — The Scaffolding Is Complete, the Wire Is Missing
ideas.description: App.jsx line 704: `const titleIdxRef = useRef(0)  // persists tab-title rotation across effect re-runs`. The comment is explicit — this ref was written to fix the known bug where `let idx = 0` resets on every 30-second poll re-run. But the title effect at lines 1064–1069 still opens with `let idx = 0` and never references `titleIdxRef`. The fix was designed, scaffolded, and abandoned. Two changes: (1) replace `let idx = 0` with `titleIdxRef.current = titleIdxRef.current % frames.length` to initialize-or-persist; (2) replace `idx` everywhere in the effect with `titleIdxRef.current`. Now `titleIdxRef` earns its existence, the comment tells the truth, and frames 1 and 2 (worker breakdown and vibe label) are visible in normal polling conditions for the first time. WHY: the ref exists, the comment exists, the bug exists — the only missing piece is the connection between them.

ideas.title: Vibe-Weather Widget Renders Its Icon Emoji Twice — Every Visitor Sees Double
ideas.description: Office.jsx lines 1567–1572: the vibe-weather widget renders two child spans — `vibe-weather__icon` containing `VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS[vibe]` (e.g., '☀️') and `vibe-weather__label` containing `VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS[vibe]` (e.g., '☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength'). `VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS` embeds the same emoji that `VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS` renders. Every sighted user sees the icon twice: `☀️ ☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength`. The `aria-hidden='true'` on the icon span correctly hides the duplication from screen readers, which makes this purely a visual mistake. Fix option A: strip the leading emoji from all `VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS` values — the icon span handles the visual, the label carries the prose. Fix option B: remove the `vibe-weather__icon` span entirely and let the label's embedded emoji serve as both. Either path ends the double-sun era. WHY: this widget is the most visible real-time data element on the office floor. It should not look broken on every single page load.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Missing startedAt — Three Reviews, One Field Away from a Live Meeting Timer
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1172: when `meetingWorkers.length >= 2`, `lastHuddle.current` is set to `{ names: meetingWorkers.map(w => w.name), endedAt: null }`. No `startedAt` field. ConferenceTable already shows a 'last huddle ended N minutes ago' retrospective when `meetingWorkers.length === 0`. Adding a live 'In huddle for Xm' badge during an active meeting requires only the start time — which is never captured. Fix: add `startedAt: Date.now()` to the object at line 1172 (only on the 0→2 transition to avoid overwriting), then pass `lastHuddle` to `ConferenceTable` for a live elapsed timer on the Huddle badge. Flagged in reviews 79, 80, and 81. Three reviews, ten characters of data, one badge that transforms a static 'Huddle' label into a living meeting clock. WHY: the office knows who is at the table and whether a huddle is happening. It just doesn't know how long they've been there. That's the one number the people outside the room are actually watching.

ideas.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS Object Allocated Inside idleWorkers.map() Callback — Fresh on Every Render
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1503: `const WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS = { carlos: [...], maya: [...], dave: [...], sofia: [...], luna: [...] }` is declared as a local const inside the `idleWorkers.map()` callback at line 1501. This object is created fresh on every call to the map callback, on every render. With 5 idle workers, 5 renders per poll, that's 5 allocations of a 5-key, 15-string object for every state update. The object is purely static — it has no dependency on worker state, vibe, or time. Fix: hoist it to module scope above the `Office` component, alongside the other character-voice constants like `WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES` and `WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES`. One cut-paste. WHY: static data declared in render paths trains developers to treat allocation costs as invisible. This one is free to fix, and hoisting it also makes the Office component's render function shorter and easier to read.

ideas.title: VIBE_RANK at Module Scope and vibeRanking Inside useEffect Are Identical Objects — DRY Violation
ideas.description: App.jsx line 389: `const VIBE_RANK = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` declared at module scope and used by `VibeSparkline` (line 398). App.jsx line 1002: `const vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` declared inline inside the vibe streak `useEffect` body for confetti logic. Same five keys, same five values, different names, different scopes, born five minutes apart in some commit. Fix: replace `const vibeRanking` at line 1002 with `const vibeRanking = VIBE_RANK` — or better, reference `VIBE_RANK` directly in the comparison at line 1004: `if (prevKey !== null && VIBE_RANK[teamVibe.key] > (VIBE_RANK[prevKey] ?? -1))`. One reference, zero duplication. WHY: two copies of the same ranking table will eventually diverge. Some future engineer will add 'hyper-crush' as a new vibe, update one map, miss the other, and spend an afternoon debugging confetti that won't fire.

ideas.title: wb-doodle Has Five Static Faces That Never React to vibeStreak — The Whiteboard Doesn't Know It's Getting Famous
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1312: `<pre className='wb-doodle'>` renders one of five static ASCII emoticons based purely on `vibe`. The Office component already receives `vibeStreak` as a prop (passed at App.jsx line 1251). A crushing sprint at streak 1 and a legendary streak-10 run look identical on the whiteboard. The whiteboard is the emotional center of the floor — it should escalate. At streak 1, crushing is calm: `╰(*°▽°*)╯`. At streak 5, it gets excited: `o(*≧▽≦)o`. At streak 10+, it goes feral: `(ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*✨`. On-fire at streak 3: table flip. On-fire at streak 8: full meltdown emoji. `vibeStreak` is already flowing through the prop — the whiteboard just needs to look at it. WHY: the streak counter in the header already shows 🔥 and 🏆 at thresholds. The whiteboard, which is larger and more central, knows nothing about it. Two narrative systems, same data, zero connection.

ideas.title: Wander Tooltip Selects by Loop Position — Carlos Changes Personality When Dave Takes a Break
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1511: `const tooltip = workerTooltips ? workerTooltips[i % workerTooltips.length] : ...` where `i` is the loop index in `idleWorkers.map()`. Carlos is index 0 when alone: gets `workerTooltips[0]` → '☕ Checking server metrics'. Carlos is index 1 when Dave is also idle first: gets `workerTooltips[1]` → '📊 Reviewing the logs'. The tooltip is derived from position in the idle queue, not from the worker's identity. Carlos's wandering thought changes based on who else got up from their desk. Fix: use a stable worker-specific index — `workerTooltips[Math.abs(w.id.split('').reduce((acc, c) => acc * 31 + c.charCodeAt(0), 0)) % workerTooltips.length]` — a hash of the worker ID that always selects the same tooltip regardless of company. WHY: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS was written with care to give each character a voice. The selector throws that care away by handing the choice to queue arithmetic.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS: Each Worker Has Been Saying the Same Sentence for 82 Reviews — Time to Give Them a Range
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 163: `PING_REACTIONS` is a flat key→string map. `luna: 'Creative review in progress...'`. Always. `carlos: 'Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.'` Always. `handleCharClick` at line 1159 calls `const reaction = PING_REACTIONS[worker.id]` — no array, no context, no randomness. The most interactive, highest-delight moment in the UI responds with a single line that becomes memorized after the second click. Replace each value with an array of 4–6 reactions, add vibe context where it matters (`sofia` at `on-fire` should be different from `sofia` at `slow-day`), and select with `Math.random()`. Example range for carlos: ['Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.', 'SIGTERM — just kidding. Probably.', 'Give me 30 seconds and a stack trace.', 'Backend is fine. Probably fine. Checking.']. This is the cheapest character-depth investment remaining in the codebase and it has been true for two consecutive reviews. WHY: click interactions are the moments users form emotional relationships with characters. One line per character communicates that the characters are statues, not people.

theme: Two real fixes, a phantom ref that declares victory without running code, and the toast twins still brawling over the same pixel
visual_observations: CONFIRMED FIXED - justShipped.forEach timer IDs: justShippedTimersRef (useRef([])) now collects all outer stagger IDs, forEach pushes each t, cleanup clears all. The zombie timer from #81 is gone.
CONFIRMED FIXED - Activity log header: header now renders 'Latest 20 of N events' when the array exceeds the cap. The silent truncation is disclosed.
STILL OPEN - plantToast (Office.jsx) and completionToast (App.jsx) both fixed at position:fixed, bottom:2rem, left:50%, zIndex:9999. Still the same pixel.
NEWLY FOUND - titleIdxRef declared at line 704 with comment 'persists tab-title rotation across effect re-runs' but line 1064 still uses let idx = 0. The ref is never read or written in the effect.
STILL OPEN - TASK_TAG_DEFS line 169: bare word 'review' in the QA pattern still stamps blue QA badge on Design review, Sprint review, Architecture review.
STILL OPEN - WallClock: CLOCK_COMMENTS live in an SVG title element on a 22px element. Hover-only on desktop, invisible on touch and screen readers.
STILL OPEN - Footer quote counter: at quoteBonus=9, counter shows 1/9 while displaying BONUS_TAGLINES[0]. Counter and content permanently phase-shifted after one full cycle.
STILL OPEN - Office.jsx line 1107: const plantStageNames declared and never referenced anywhere. The array is an orphan.
NEWLY FOUND - ProgressBar call site (App.jsx line 350) still passes updatedAt={worker.updated_at} as a dead prop after updatedAt was removed from the function signature.

fixed_since_last_review.title: justShipped.forEach Outer setTimeout IDs Now Collected and Cleared
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx: justShippedTimersRef = useRef([]) added. forEach now pushes each outer setTimeout ID into the ref array. Effect cleanup calls justShippedTimersRef.current.forEach(clearTimeout) and resets to []. Multi-ship no longer leaves zombie timers in stale state.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Activity Log Header Now Accurately Discloses Latest 20 of N Events
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx dashboard section: header renders 'Latest N of M events' when filtered.length > DISPLAY_CAP, and 'N of M events (filtered)' when filters are active. The silent truncation that showed 50 events while displaying only 20 is closed.

ideas.title: plantToast and completionToast Still Overlap at bottom:2rem - One Erases the Other
ideas.description: Office.jsx and App.jsx both anchor toasts at position:fixed, bottom:2rem, left:50%, zIndex:9999. A ficus level-up and a task completion in the same polling window produce two toasts at the exact same pixel and one is invisible. Fix: stagger vertical positions (plant at bottom:5.5rem, completion at bottom:2rem) or introduce a shared toast queue with a 200ms gap. The easiest character moment on screen requires the toast to actually be visible.

ideas.title: titleIdxRef Declared with 'Persists Rotation' Comment but Never Wired to the Title Effect
ideas.description: App.jsx line 704: const titleIdxRef = useRef(0) with comment 'persists tab-title rotation across effect re-runs'. Line 1064: let idx = 0 still resets on every re-run. titleIdxRef is never read or written in the title effect - a placeholder that impersonates a fix. Wire it: replace the local let idx with the ref inside the effect and update titleIdxRef.current instead of idx throughout. Frames 1 and 2 will finally rotate in practice.

ideas.title: TASK_TAG_DEFS Bare 'review' Still Stamps QA Badge on Design and Sprint Reviews
ideas.description: App.jsx TASK_TAG_DEFS line 169: the QA pattern includes bare word 'review', matching 'Design review', 'Sprint review', 'Architecture review', 'Creative review in progress'. All four get the blue QA badge. Fix: qualify the pattern to require context - replace bare 'review' with qualified anchors like 'code review' or 'peer review'. Removes false positives on planning ceremonies without losing legitimate detection. A wrong badge on 'Sprint review' breaks trust in the entire tagging system.

ideas.title: Footer Quote Counter Phase-Shifts Permanently After One Full Cycle
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1410: counter formula uses (quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1. At quoteBonus=9 with length=8: (9%9)+1=1, showing '1/9', while content is BONUS_TAGLINES[0] - first bonus tagline, not the vibe tagline. Counter says slot 1 but displays slot 2 content. After one full cycle the counter and content are permanently phase-shifted. Fix: reset quoteBonus to 0 at wrap so the vibe tagline genuinely occupies the reset state and counter only renders for quoteBonus >= 1.

ideas.title: WallClock Vibe Commentary Buried in SVG title Element - Touch Devices and Screen Readers Never See It
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1050: five vibe-specific CLOCK_COMMENTS live inside an SVG title element on a 22x22px icon. Hover-only on desktop, completely inaccessible on touch and screen readers. 'You have checked this three times in the last hour' and 'Time has no meaning during an incident' are the most human lines in the codebase and most users never read them. Fix: surface the comment as a visible tooltip or caption appearing on focus or hover with role='tooltip' and proper association. The writing deserves to be read.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Call Site Still Passes Dead updatedAt Prop After Signature Cleanup
ideas.description: App.jsx line 195: function ProgressBar({ progress, startedAt }) - updatedAt was removed from the signature. Line 350: still passes updatedAt={worker.updated_at} at the call site, silently discarded by React. The cleanup is half-done. Fix: remove updatedAt from the call site, or better - add it back to the signature and use it: show a stall indicator when progress < 100 and updatedAt is older than 30 minutes. The data is there; it would surface stalled tasks without waiting for the 2-hour stuck threshold.

ideas.title: After-Hours Greeting Alternates on Even/Odd Clock Hour - Clock Parity Is Not Team Awareness
ideas.description: App.jsx line 1106: currentHour % 2 === 0 produces 'Burning the midnight oil' at hours 20 and 22, and 'Still at it' at hours 21 and 23 - mechanically alternating by clock parity. A team crushing at 10pm gets the same rotation as a team idling at 3am. Fix: select the after-hours greeting based on teamVibe.key: crushing/on-fire = 'Burning the midnight oil', slow-day/after-hours = 'Still at it', in-flow = 'Late-night flow state'. The greeting and the vibe should agree.

ideas.title: plantStageNames Array Still Declared Inside Office Component and Still Referenced Nowhere
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1107: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] declared in the hot-path component body, never read. plantStageLabel and plantStageEmoji are both built inline using the stage string directly. Flagged in #81, still present. Fix: remove it or wire it to a PLANT_GROWTH_MESSAGES map keyed by stage and use it in the plantToast so each of the five ficus transitions gets its own line.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Still Missing startedAt - Live Meeting Duration Architecturally Blocked Since #81
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 1174: lastHuddle.current = { names, endedAt: null } with no startedAt field. The Huddle badge shows how long ago a meeting ended but cannot show how long the current one has been running. Fix: capture startedAt on the 0-to-2+ worker transition and display 'in meeting 14m' on the live Huddle badge. Meeting duration is the most interesting signal on the conference table. The table is decorative without it.

ideas.title: Ficus Toast Still Says the Same Generic Line for All Five Stage Transitions

The team is thriving.', blooming = 'Peak ficus. The office smells incredible.' Three lines of code, five distinct moments.

NEWLY FOUND - ProgressBar milestone useEffect (App.jsx ~line 205): two compounding bugs. First: if progress jumps past multiple milestones in one poll (e.g., 10 to 80), only the 25% flash fires because return inside the for-loop short-circuits on the lowest uncelebrated threshold. The 50%, 75%, and completion animations are swallowed. Second: if any progress update arrives within 600ms of hitting a milestone, React's cleanup runs clearTimeout(t), but the new effect run finds no new milestone and never calls setFlashClass('') -- the flash CSS class is permanently stuck until the next milestone fires or progress resets.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Flash Class Gets Permanently Stuck When Progress Updates Within 600ms of a Milestone
ideas.description: App.jsx ProgressBar useEffect: when a milestone (25/50/75/100%) is crossed, setFlashClass is called, a 600ms setTimeout clears it, and return () => clearTimeout(t) registers the cleanup. If any progress update arrives before those 600ms elapse, React runs the cleanup (cancelling the timeout) then runs the new effect -- which finds no new milestone and exits the for loop silently, leaving flashClass permanently set to 'progress-milestone-25' (or whichever). The bar glows indefinitely. Fix: call setFlashClass('') unconditionally at the top of the effect before re-checking milestones so it always resets on every run, then re-trigger the flash if a threshold is crossed.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Milestone Loop Short-Circuits on Lowest Threshold -- 100% Jump Shows 25% Flash
ideas.description: App.jsx ProgressBar useEffect: MILESTONES = [25, 50, 75, 100] iterated ascending. The return statement inside the loop exits on the first uncelebrated hit. If progress jumps from 10% to 100% in one API poll, only the 25% flash fires. The 50%, 75%, and 100% milestones are skipped. The completion animation is dropped every time a task progresses faster than one API cycle per 25 points. Fix: iterate in descending order so the highest meaningful milestone fires, not the lowest noise threshold.

ideas.title: Ficus Grief Toast — No Code Path Exists for Plant Regression
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1158-1164 — the useEffect fires a toast when plantStage changes AND plantStage !== 'seedling'. When the streak drops and plantStage returns to 'seedling', the condition is false and nothing happens. The ref updates correctly (line 1165 reached on fall-through), so the diagnosis is NOT a stuck ref — it's a missing else-if branch. Add: `else if (prevPlantStageRef.current !== plantStage && prevPlantStageRef.current !== 'seedling' && plantStage === 'seedling') { setPlantToast('grief'); const t = setTimeout(() => setPlantToast(false), 3500); return () => clearTimeout(t) }`. Add a grief entry to PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES: `grief: '🥀 The office ficus withered. The streak is gone.'`. WHY: the plant growing has fanfare. The plant dying has silence. That asymmetry makes the streak decoration feel disposable. Thirteen reviews — correct diagnosis this time.

ideas.title: plantStageNames Is a Dead Variable — Orphan From an Incomplete Refactor
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1137 — const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] is declared in the component body and referenced nowhere. The stage names are used inline at line 1138-1141 and in PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES keys. This array is an artifact. Delete the line. WHY: dead variables are cognitive noise. Every developer who reads the ficus section will wonder what plantStageNames was for and whether removing it breaks something. It doesn't.

ideas.title: WallClock Vibe Comments Trapped Inside SVG title — Touch-Invisible
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1063-1080 — five beautifully written vibe-specific clock comments ('You have checked this three times in the last hour', 'Time has no meaning during an incident') exist only inside a <title> element on a 22x22px SVG. This means: (1) hover-only on desktop, (2) completely invisible on mobile/touch, (3) not exposed to screen readers in a meaningful way. These are some of the best copy in the entire codebase and almost nobody will ever read them. Fix: add an aria-describedby tooltip that renders on focus/hover with a visible tooltip element, OR render the comment as a small label below the clock on hover using CSS :hover + sibling selector. WHY: content that exists but can never be discovered is worse than content that doesn't exist — it represents effort that delivers zero value.

ideas.title: completionToast Is Generic — '🎉 NAME shipped it!' for Every Worker Every Time
ideas.description: App.jsx:1161 — the toast checks completionToast.text first (used only for streak eulogy) then falls back to '🎉 ${name} shipped it!'. Carlos shipping a bug fix at 2am gets the same line as Maya shipping a design component during a crushing sprint. The .text path already exists — use it. Define WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES keyed by worker.id with 3-4 options each: carlos: ['📊 Carlos closed it. The logs are clean.', '🔧 Carlos shipped. stdout agrees.'], maya: ['💜 Maya shipped — pixel-perfect as always.', '🎨 Maya delivered. The spacing is correct.'], etc. Pick randomly at ship time. WHY: the toast is 3 seconds of peak emotional attention. Generic copy wastes the moment.

ideas.title: Door Icon Ignores DOOR_LABELS Emoji — Three States for Five Vibes
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1508 — DOOR_LABELS contains vibe-keyed entries with emoji ('🚀 All hands in', '🧯 Brace yourself') but the door icon itself is always 🚪 (with ✨ on arrival, 🔒 after-hours). The emoji in DOOR_LABELS only appears in the text label below. Add a DOOR_ICONS map: crushing → '🚪💨', on-fire → '🚨', in-flow → '🚪', slow-day → '☁️🚪', after-hours → '🔒'. Use it for the icon so the door communicates vibe at a glance. WHY: the door is the first architectural element visitors notice in the office. Right now it's emotionally flat.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS Should Be Vibe-Aware — Same Worker, Different Energy
ideas.description: Office.jsx:163-200 — PING_REACTIONS is now a 5-string array per worker (great fix!). But the pick is random regardless of vibe. Carlos during 'on-fire' should say something different than Carlos during 'slow-day'. Structure as { carlos: { crushing: [...], 'on-fire': [...], default: [...] } }. The ping is the most personal interaction in the UI — it should reflect the room's mood. WHY: vibe-awareness is the thread that makes the dashboard feel alive. The greeting, footer, whiteboard, and coffee corner are all vibe-reactive. The ping responses are the one holdout.

theme: The tab-title fix has a hole in it — visibility change explicitly resets to idx=0 — plus ProgressBar is still wearing a flash badge it earned two months ago, and the quote counter silently laps itself at 8/8
visual_observations: Puppeteer timed out in container. Full code audit of App.jsx (1418 lines), Office.jsx (1642 lines), App.css (1165 lines), Office.css (2406 lines).

CONFIRMED FIXED — Ficus grief toast: Office.jsx:1165-1169 — the else-if branch exists, fires setPlantToast('grief') when plantStage regresses to seedling, and PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES has a 'grief' key at line 1152. Fourteen reviews of diagnosis, finally landed.
CONFIRMED FIXED — plantStageNames dead variable: absent from Office.jsx. No trace in grep output. Removed clean.
CONFIRMED FIXED — ProgressBar updatedAt dead prop: function signature is now { progress, startedAt } at App.jsx:196. Call site at App.jsx:351 only passes progress and startedAt.
CONFIRMED FIXED — PING_REACTIONS now 5-string arrays per worker: Office.jsx:163-200. Five distinct lines per character. Click variety confirmed.
CONFIRMED FIXED — justShipped zombie timers: justShippedTimersRef.current.forEach(t => clearTimeout(t)) at App.jsx:958-959.
CONFIRMED FIXED — plantToast and completionToast staggered: plantToast at bottom:5.5rem (Office.jsx:1295), completionToast at bottom:2rem (App.jsx:1141). No overlap.
CONFIRMED FIXED — QA tag pattern excludes bare 'review': pattern is now /test|qa|spec|code.?review|coverage|lint/i at App.jsx:169.
CONFIRMED FIXED — Footer counter display capped: Math.min(quoteBonus, BONUS_TAGLINES.length) at App.jsx:1412. Counter does not exceed 8/8.
CONFIRMED FIXED — ActivityLog header count accurate: 'Latest 20 of N events' at App.jsx:1341.

NEW BUG — Tab title visibility handler resets to idx=0: App.jsx:1083 — when the tab becomes visible again, the handleVisibilityChange else branch sets `idx = 0` explicitly. titleIdxRef.current is updated inside the interval but IGNORED on tab return. The fix that landed (titleIdxRef.current persists across effect re-runs) only works between poll cycles, not on tab focus. Every time a user switches back to the tab, the rotation restarts from frame 0.

STILL OPEN — ProgressBar milestone loop early return: App.jsx:207-214 — the for loop has `return () => clearTimeout(t)` inside it, which exits on the first triggered milestone. If progress jumps from 0 to 100 in one render cycle, only the 25% milestone flash fires. The 50, 75, and 100 milestones are silently swallowed.

STILL OPEN — ProgressBar flash class permanently stuck: App.jsx:207-214 — the effect cleanup runs `clearTimeout(t)` which cancels the scheduled `setFlashClass('')` call. If progress updates again within 600ms of crossing a milestone, the cleanup from the new effect run cancels the clear-timer, leaving the milestone flash class applied indefinitely. The progress bar stays highlighted as 'progress-milestone-25' (or whatever the first milestone was) forever until the next milestone is crossed.

STILL OPEN — lastHuddle missing startedAt: Office.jsx:1200 — lastHuddle.current is initialized as { names: [], endedAt: null }. When a meeting starts at line 1218, it sets { names: [...], endedAt: null } but never records startedAt. ConferenceTable receives this ref and shows post-meeting elapsed time (line 951-957), but there is no way to show live meeting duration while the meeting is active because the start time was never recorded.

STILL OPEN — WallClock vibe comments inside SVG title: Office.jsx:1080 — five beautiful vibe-aware comments (Peak hours. Nobody watches the clock except the clock.; Time has no meaning during an incident.; You have checked this three times in the last hour.; Do not look. It breaks the flow.; Still here? So am I.) live inside a <title> element on a 22x22px SVG. Desktop hover only. Invisible on touch. Not announced by screen readers in a useful context.

NEW BUG — Quote counter deceptive after full cycle: App.jsx:1412 — BONUS_TAGLINES has 8 entries. After quoteBonus reaches 9, the counter display is capped at 8/8 by Math.min (correct), but the tagline index wraps silently back to BONUS[0] via (quoteBonus - 1) % 8. The counter reads '8/8' implying the user has seen everything, while the quote displayed is actually the first bonus tagline again. The user has no way to know the carousel restarted.

PERFORMANCE NOTE — CLOCK_COMMENTS defined inside WallClock on every render: Office.jsx:1063-1069 — a 5-key object literal is allocated inside the WallClock function body. WallClock re-renders every minute (via the setInterval at line 1042). This is a static map with no dependency on props or state — it should be a module-level constant. Not a visible bug, but unnecessary allocation on every clock tick.

PERFORMANCE NOTE — PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES defined inside Office component body: Office.jsx:1145-1152 — a 5-key object allocated inside the Office render function on every render. Office re-renders on any worker status change. This map is entirely static and should live at module scope.

STILL OPEN — completionToast is worker-blind: App.jsx:1161 — `completionToast.text ? completionToast.text : '🎉 ${completionToast.name} shipped it!'`. The .text path is used only for the streak eulogy. Every actual completion toast is identical across all five workers and all five vibes.

STILL OPEN — DOOR_LABELS emoji unreachable: Office.jsx:1511-1514 — the door icon element is always '🚪', '🚪✨' on arrival, or '🔒' after-hours. DOOR_LABELS at line 139 contains emoji-prefixed strings ('🚀 All hands in', '🧯 Brace yourself', etc.) but those emoji only appear in the text label below the icon. The icon itself is vibe-blind.

fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:1165-1169 — else-if branch fires setPlantToast('grief') when plantStage === 'seedling' after regression. PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES['grief'] = '🥀 The office ficus withered. The streak is gone.' The plant now mourns.

ideas.title: Tab Title Visibility Handler Explicitly Resets idx to 0 — titleIdxRef.current Ignored on Tab Return
ideas.description: App.jsx:1083 — the handleVisibilityChange else branch (tab becomes visible) sets `idx = 0` then calls `document.title = frames[idx]`. titleIdxRef.current is only updated inside the interval at App.jsx:1069, but is never read back in the visibility handler. Fix: replace `idx = 0` with `idx = titleIdxRef.current`. This is a one-character fix that completes the original intent. WHY: the partial fix only solved persistence across poll cycles. The user's most common interaction — switching away and back — still resets the rotation to frame 0 every single time. The ref is updated but never consulted at the one moment it matters.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Flash Class Permanently Stuck — Effect Cleanup Cancels Its Own Clear-Timer
ideas.description: App.jsx:207-214 — the effect sets `const t = setTimeout(() => setFlashClass(''), 600)` then returns `() => clearTimeout(t)`. React calls the cleanup before running the next effect. If `progress` updates again within 600ms (i.e., any poll that happens to hit during the flash window), the cleanup fires FIRST, cancels t, and `setFlashClass('')` never runs. The milestone flash class stays applied to the progress bar indefinitely. Fix: do NOT return the clearTimeout as a cleanup for this pattern. Use a ref to track the timer: `flashTimerRef.current = setTimeout(...)` and in the cleanup do `clearTimeout(flashTimerRef.current)` ONLY if you also set a new flash, otherwise let the timer fire. Or better: move the flash logic into a useReducer that auto-expires, eliminating the timeout/cleanup race entirely. WHY: a permanently highlighted progress bar is visually misleading — it implies a milestone is happening when the worker has actually been idle for 30 minutes.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Milestone Loop Early-Returns on First Hit — Multi-Threshold Jumps Show Only 25% Flash
ideas.description: App.jsx:207-214 — the for loop iterates MILESTONES [25, 50, 75, 100] and on the first untripped threshold, adds the milestone, sets the flash class, schedules a clear-timer, then `return () => clearTimeout(t)`. This return exits the loop. If progress goes from 0 to 100 in one render (or 0 to 76), only the 25% flash fires. Fix: break the loop into two passes — first pass identifies ALL newly crossed milestones and adds them to the set; second pass fires only the highest one (or queue them with staggered delays). The return should only exist as the effect cleanup, outside the for loop. WHY: crossing 100% on a Friday afternoon should show a 100% flash, not a 25% flash. The current behavior is quietly wrong and the milestone animations are essentially non-functional for any worker that starts with high initial progress.

ideas.title: Quote Counter Shows 8/8 But Silently Wraps — Counter Is Dishonest After Full Cycle
ideas.description: App.jsx:1412 — BONUS_TAGLINES has 8 entries. Once quoteBonus hits 9, the counter clamps to 8/8 via Math.min, but (quoteBonus - 1) % 8 wraps back to index 0. The quote shown is the first bonus tagline again, but the counter says '8/8' as if the user has seen them all. Two fixes possible: (1) once quoteBonus > BONUS_TAGLINES.length, show '1/8' again and indicate the wrap: counter = `${((quoteBonus - 1) % BONUS_TAGLINES.length) + 1}/${BONUS_TAGLINES.length}` unconditionally. (2) Add a '🔁' or '(cycling)' indicator after the counter when quoteBonus > BONUS_TAGLINES.length. WHY: a counter that reads 8/8 while showing quote #1 is telling a lie. The user clicks expecting a new quote and gets one they already saw with no indication that the list has looped. It reads as a bug, not a feature.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Missing startedAt — Live Meeting Duration Architecturally Blocked
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1200 — lastHuddle is initialized as `{ names: [], endedAt: null }`. When a meeting starts at line 1218, only `names` is recorded. When it ends at line 1220, `endedAt` is set. The ConferenceTable component uses lastHuddle only to show post-meeting elapsed time. There is no live 'meeting in progress for X minutes' display because startedAt was never stored. Fix: add `startedAt: Date.now()` to the object at line 1218. Then in ConferenceTable, when `meetingWorkers.length >= 2`, show `Meeting: ${Math.floor((Date.now() - h.startedAt) / 60000)}m and counting`. WHY: the conference table knows who's in the meeting but not how long they've been there. A 4-person meeting that's been running 90 minutes and a 4-person meeting that just started look identical. Duration is the most useful signal.

ideas.title: WallClock Vibe Comments Still Trapped in SVG title — Best Copy in the Codebase, Zero Reach
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1080 — `<title>{displayH}:{displayM} ... — {CLOCK_COMMENTS[vibeKey]}</title>`. Five vibe-specific lines (Peak hours. Nobody watches the clock except the clock.; You have checked this three times in the last hour.; Still here? So am I. I am a clock. That is fine for me.) live exclusively inside an SVG title element on a 22x22px icon. Touch devices: never. Screen readers: technically accessible but requires screen reader to announce SVG titles, which many don't by default. Fix: render a visible tooltip or footnote below the clock on hover/focus using a CSS ::after pseudo-element or a sibling div with pointer-events:none. Or surface the comment as a 1-line aria-describedby tooltip. WHY: this is the third review flagging this. The clock comments are the most personality-dense writing in the entire project. They are functionally unreachable to the majority of users.

ideas.title: CLOCK_COMMENTS and PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES Defined Inside Component Bodies — Allocated on Every Render
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1063-1069 — CLOCK_COMMENTS is a 5-key object defined inside the WallClock function body. WallClock re-renders every minute via setInterval. Office.jsx:1145-1152 — PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES is a 5-key object defined inside the Office component body. Office re-renders on any worker status change. Both are entirely static maps with no dependency on props or state. Hoist both to module scope (above the respective function declarations). WHY: not a visible bug, but object allocation inside hot render paths is unnecessary cognitive noise and a micro-performance cost that compounds with render frequency. The fix is a single-line move per constant.

ideas.title: completionToast Still Worker-Blind — '🎉 NAME shipped it!' for All Five Workers, All Five Vibes
ideas.description: App.jsx:1161 — `completionToast.text ? completionToast.text : '🎉 ${completionToast.name} shipped it!'`. The .text path only fires for streak eulogy. Every actual work-completion toast is the same sentence, same structure, same emoji. Define WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES keyed by worker ID with 3-4 rotating options: carlos: ['📊 Carlos closed it. The logs are clean.', '🔧 Carlos shipped. stdout agrees.'], maya: ['💜 Maya shipped — pixel-perfect as always.', '🎨 Maya delivered. The spacing is correct.'], dave: ['🎧 Dave shipped. The pipeline agrees.'], sofia: ['🔍 Sofia shipped. Bug count: 0.', '🧪 Sofia shipped. Coverage held.'], luna: ['✨ Luna shipped. The brief was beautiful.', '🌙 Luna delivered. The moodboard lives.']. Pick by index using Date.now() % array.length at ship time. WHY: the 3-second toast is the most emotionally loaded moment in the UI — it celebrates a real work event. Generic copy is a missed opportunity to make each worker feel distinct.

ideas.title: DOOR_LABELS Emoji Is Decoration-Only — Door Icon Stays 🚪 Regardless of Vibe
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1511-1514 — the door icon element renders: `vibe === 'after-hours' ? '🔒' : doorEvent === 'arrive' ? '🚪✨' : '🚪'`. Three states for five vibes. DOOR_LABELS at line 139 already contains vibe-keyed strings with emoji prefixes ('🚀 All hands in', '🧯 Brace yourself', '⚡ Focus zone', '☁️ Come on in', '🔒 Locked up') but those emoji only appear in the text label, not the icon. Either separate the emoji from the label strings (DOOR_LABEL_ICONS object keyed by vibe) or use the first character of DOOR_LABELS[vibe] for the icon. WHY: the door is the entry-point visual that sets the office mood. Right now it communicates arrival/departure (via doorEvent) but not atmosphere. A '🌩️' door during on-fire and a '☀️' door during crushing would read immediately without requiring a user to read the text label.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS Not Vibe-Aware — Carlos During an Incident Says the Same Thing as Carlos on a Slow Day
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1205-1213 — PING_REACTIONS is an object with 5-string arrays per worker. The reaction is picked randomly (`reactions[Math.floor(Math.random() * reactions.length)]`). The current vibe is never consulted. Carlos during 'on-fire' gives the same five responses as Carlos during 'slow-day'. Restructure as `{ carlos: { 'on-fire': [...], 'slow-day': [...], default: [...] } }`. In handleCharClick, resolve by `PING_REACTIONS[worker.id][vibe] ?? PING_REACTIONS[worker.id]['default']`. Carlos on-fire could say 'Do not ping me. I am in the logs. The logs are ON FIRE.' Carlos slow-day: 'Nothing in the logs. That is the problem.' WHY: the ping is the most personal interaction in the UI. Vibe-awareness here would make the whole dashboard feel like a living system rather than a static display with some click handlers. Every other layer (whiteboard, weather, footer quote, door label, bloberto face) is already vibe-reactive. Pings are the one holdout.

fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: plantStageNames array absent from Office.jsx. Grep returns zero results. Dead orphan gone.

fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:163-199 — each of luna, carlos, maya, dave, sofia has five distinct reaction lines. handleCharClick at line 1207 picks randomly. Characters no longer respond with a single static string.

ideas.title: Tab Title Inner setInterval Does Not Write titleIdxRef.current — Second Hide-Show Reads Stale Index
ideas.description: App.jsx:1067-1098. The outer setInterval correctly writes titleIdxRef.current = idx on every tick (line 1069). The inner setInterval created inside the visibilitychange else-branch (lines 1093-1096) only updates local idx and never writes back to titleIdxRef.current. After the first hide+show cycle the outer interval is gone. When the user hides and returns a second time, line 1091 reads a stale ref from the first hide. Fix: add titleIdxRef.current = idx to the inner interval body.

ideas.title: ProgressBar Milestone Loop Returns Early — Only First Crossed Threshold Ever Flashes
ideas.description: App.jsx:207-213. The for-loop over MILESTONES contains a cleanup return inside the first matching branch. In React useEffect a returned function is the cleanup function — returning from inside the loop exits useEffect after the first hit. If progress jumps from 0 to 100 in one poll cycle, only the 25pct flash fires; 50, 75, and 100 are silently skipped. Fix: collect all crossed milestones, schedule all timers, then return a single cleanup.

ideas.title: CLOCK_COMMENTS and PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES Defined Inside Component Bodies — Per-Render Allocation
ideas.description: WallClock at Office.jsx:1063 and the Office function at Office.jsx:1145 — both are pure static objects with no dependency on props or state. Every render allocates a fresh object reference. Move both to module scope. This matters most for PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES since Office re-renders on every worker status or vibe change, rebuilding the map on every poll cycle.

ideas.title: completionToast Is Worker-Blind — Same Copy for Everyone, Every Vibe
ideas.description: App.jsx:1161. The fallback toast text is identical for all five workers across all five vibes. Worker name and ROLE_COLORS are already in scope at lines 964-966 when the toast is scheduled. Carlos shipping a database migration during on-fire should read differently from Luna wrapping up a review on a slow day. Add a COMPLETION_TOASTS map keyed by worker.id with optional vibe sub-keys, falling back to the generic line only when no specific copy exists. Five workers times two tiers equals ten lines — a one-afternoon spec task for luna, one-hour implementation for carlos.

ideas.title: Door Icon Ignores DOOR_LABELS — 4 of 5 Vibes Show the Generic Door Emoji
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1513. The icon slot renders after-hours as locked, doorEvent arrive as door-sparkle, and everything else as generic door. DOOR_LABELS at lines 139-145 already has per-vibe strings with leading emoji (rocket, fire-extinguisher, lightning, cloud, lock). The leading emoji was clearly intended as the icon but is never extracted. Either split into DOOR_ICONS and DOOR_LABEL_TEXT, or take the first character of DOOR_LABELS[vibe] as the icon. One-line fix — all design decisions already made.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS Not Vibe-Aware — Same Worker, Same Lines Regardless of Team State
ideas.description: Office.jsx:163-199. The five-string arrays are an improvement but still static. handleCharClick at line 1207 ignores current vibe when selecting a reaction. Carlos during an incident should reference the chaos; Carlos on a slow day should be philosophical. Structure PING_REACTIONS as workerId -> vibeKey -> array with a default fallback, and let handleCharClick pick from the vibe-specific pool first. Even two tiers (default and on-fire) makes characters feel present in the moment.

ideas.title: WallClock CLOCK_COMMENTS Still Locked in SVG title — Best Copy Has Zero Touch Reach
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1080. The vibe comment is rendered only as an SVG title attribute, surfacing as a native browser tooltip on desktop hover only. Touch users never see it. Lines like Time has no meaning during an incident and You have checked this three times in the last hour are some of the sharpest copy in the codebase. Options: show the comment as a visible caption on tap or click of the clock, feed it into Bloberto speech bubble queue as a low-frequency injection, or render it in a clock hover-card similar to the worker char-hover-card pattern already in use.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Missing startedAt — Live Meeting Duration Badge Architecturally Blocked
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1218. lastHuddle.current is set to { names, endedAt: null } when a huddle begins. No startedAt is recorded. A live badge showing Huddle duration in progress is impossible without it. The fix is one line: add startedAt: Date.now() when the huddle opens at line 1218. Then ConferenceTable can compute elapsed time and show live duration alongside the existing Huddle badge.

ideas.title: Quote Counter Shows N/N Forever After Full Cycle — Display Lies After 8 Clicks
ideas.description: App.jsx:1412. Math.min(quoteBonus, BONUS_TAGLINES.length) caps the display at 8/8 but quotes continue cycling via (quoteBonus - 1) mod 8. After the 8th click the counter freezes at 8/8 while content keeps changing — it implies completion but the quotes keep looping. Simplest fix: cap quoteBonus at BONUS_TAGLINES.length so clicking beyond 8 wraps silently and the dishonest counter disappears after the first full cycle.

ideas.title: Bloberto Mood Ring — Ambient Team-Health Signal Encoded in a Single Pulse
ideas.description: Bloberto has a face, speech bubble, syncing indicator, and title badge but no persistent real-time health signal visible without interaction. Propose a soft animated ring around Bloberto avatar whose color tracks vibe, pulse speed tracks active worker ratio (fast when all hands on deck, slow when team idles), and glow intensity is inverse of error count (bright means clean, dimming means errors rising). During crushing with no errors: fast bright purple pulse. During on-fire with 2 errors: slow flickering red. All signals available from existing props with no new data fetching. Maya owns CSS keyframes; luna specs the threshold values and timing curve. A single element that tells the whole story, visible at a glance.

theme: The big bugs are gone — PING_REACTIONS speaks, toasts no longer collide, the ficus has stage-specific messages, and DOOR_ICONS works. What remains is polish: double-emoji weather, completion toast still a form letter, and the clock's best prose locked behind hover.

CONFIRMED FIXED — plantToast renders at bottom:5.5rem, completionToast at bottom:2rem. Toast overlap resolved.
CONFIRMED FIXED — PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES map exists at Office.jsx:1146, keyed by stage name. Each ficus transition gets its own message. plantToast passes the stage string, not a boolean.
CONFIRMED FIXED — DOOR_ICONS map at Office.jsx:147. The door icon now renders per-vibe emoji via DOOR_ICONS[vibe]. Four vibes no longer show the generic door.
CONFIRMED FIXED — titleIdxRef.current is now used in the title effect (App.jsx:~1075). idx persists across re-runs. Frames 1 and 2 are visible during normal polling.
CONFIRMED FIXED — Footer quote counter: quoteBonus capped at BONUS_TAGLINES.length (onClick uses p < length ? p+1 : p). No more wrap-around phase shift.

STILL OPEN — VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS (Office.jsx:108) embed emoji in every value. VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS (line 115) has the same emoji. The render at line 1638-1639 shows both spans — double emoji on every vibe. Sun-sun, storm-storm, cloud-cloud.

STILL OPEN — completionToast (App.jsx:1169): completionToast.text is the new field but grep shows no WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES constant or vibe-aware text generation. The .text field may be set generically. Need to verify if character voice is actually wired.
STILL OPEN — WallClock CLOCK_COMMENTS still inside SVG title element. Touch devices and screen readers never see these lines.
STILL OPEN — lastHuddle.current still set without startedAt. Live meeting elapsed time remains blocked.
