ideas.title: Vibe-Weather Widget Renders Double Emoji — Icon Span and Label Both Carry the Same Symbol
ideas.description: Office.jsx lines 108-122 and 1638-1639: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS values all start with an emoji (e.g. "☀️ Clear skies..."). VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS has the same emoji rendered in a separate span with aria-hidden. Every visitor sees ☀️ ☀️ Clear skies, ⛈️ ⛈️ Storm conditions, etc. Fix: strip the leading emoji from all VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS values since the icon span already handles the visual. Or remove the icon span and let the label embed handle both. Two minutes of find-and-replace. WHY: double emoji looks like a rendering bug, not a design choice.

ideas.title: completionToast Still Falls Back to Generic "shipped it!" — Worker Voice Not Yet Wired
ideas.description: App.jsx:1169: renders completionToast.text if present, otherwise falls back to the generic "🎉 name shipped it!" line. The .text field was added as infrastructure but no WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES constant or vibe-aware text generator populates it. The 3-second toast at maximum visibility — the highest-emotion moment in the UI — still sounds like a CI webhook for most or all workers. Define a WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES map keyed by worker ID with 3-4 character-voice lines, pick randomly when setting completionToast. WHY: the toast is the applause moment. It should sound like someone who knows the worker, not a deployment script.

ideas.title: WallClock CLOCK_COMMENTS Trapped in SVG title — Touch Users and Screen Readers Never See Them
ideas.description: Office.jsx ~line 1050: five vibe-specific CLOCK_COMMENTS live inside an SVG title element on a 22px icon. Desktop hover shows a native tooltip. Touch devices show nothing. Screen readers get the title but most users never discover it. These are the most human lines in the codebase ("You have checked this three times in the last hour", "Time has no meaning during an incident"). Surface them as a visible tooltip on hover/focus with role=tooltip and proper aria association, or render as a small caption below the clock. WHY: the best writing in the app should be readable by more than desktop-hover power users.

ideas.title: lastHuddle Still Missing startedAt — Live Meeting Duration Remains Blocked
ideas.description: Office.jsx ~line 1174: lastHuddle.current = { names, endedAt: null } with no startedAt. ConferenceTable shows retrospective "ended X ago" but cannot show "in meeting for Xm" during a live huddle. Add startedAt: Date.now() on the 0→2+ worker transition. The one number people outside the room want to know is how long the meeting has been going. WHY: the conference table is the most prominent shared-space element and it cannot answer the most basic question about an active meeting.

ideas.title: VIBE_RANK and vibeRanking Are the Same 5-Key Object Defined Twice
ideas.description: App.jsx line ~389: VIBE_RANK at module scope. Line ~1002: vibeRanking inside a useEffect body. Identical keys and values. Replace the inner vibeRanking with a direct reference to VIBE_RANK. WHY: two copies will diverge when someone adds a new vibe and updates one but not the other.

ideas.title: Bloberto Mood Ring — Team Health Encoded in a Subtle Ambient Pulse
ideas.description: The Bloberto character on the office floor could have a subtle ambient CSS animation — a slow glow or pulse — whose color and speed derive from the current vibe and streak. Crushing+streak5 = warm gold steady pulse. On-fire = fast red flicker. Slow-day = barely visible blue breathe. After-hours = dim purple heartbeat. No new data, no new state — just CSS keyframes driven by data-vibe and data-streak attributes already on the office-floor div. WHY: the manager character should visually reflect the team state without any text. A living pulse makes Bloberto feel like a creature, not an avatar.

theme: Nine things shipped, two constants living double lives, and one toast that still doesn't know anyone's name.
visual_observations: ProgressBar now collects ALL crossed milestones into a crossed[] array and schedules staggered timers — no more early-return on first hit. The confetti sequence actually plays.
CLOCK_COMMENTS and PLANT_STAGE_MESSAGES both hoisted to module scope. No more per-render object allocation inside their respective components.
lastHuddle.startedAt is captured at Office.jsx:1241. ConferenceTable renders '· Xm' inside the Huddle badge using a 30-second setInterval tick. Live meeting timer is real.
DOOR_ICONS defined at Office.jsx:147 with all five vibe keys; used cleanly in the door render. The door finally knows its own icon.
PING_REACTIONS now has 5 character-specific strings per worker. Carlos talks about logs exclusively. Sofia talks about edge cases exclusively. Character voices are consistent.
Ficus grief toast fires via else-if branch at Office.jsx:1165 when vibeStreak regresses. The plant has feelings now.
VIBE_WEATHER double emoji still present: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS embeds the emoji in the string ('☀️ Clear skies…') AND VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS renders the same emoji in a separate span. Users see it twice.

WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS is still declared inside idleWorkers.map() at Office.jsx:1571 — a fresh object on every render per idle worker. Content is character-specific (good). Allocation location is not (bad).
completionToast still resolves to '🎉 NAME shipped it!' for all five workers at App.jsx:1169. Carlos ships a deploy fix and Maya ships a pixel spec — same toast, different universe.
VIBE_RANK defined at App.jsx:397 (module scope). Identical vibeRanking object redefined inside a useEffect body at App.jsx:1010. Two sources of truth for the same ranking.
Quote counter now hides when quoteBonus reaches BONUS_TAGLINES.length. The 8th quote is the last — no wrap, no bounce, the counter just disappears. Silent completion with no celebration.
Conference table ambient label shows plain 'locked up' for after-hours while DOOR_LABELS uses '🔒 Locked up'. Inconsistent polish between two adjacent components.

ideas.title: completionToast Doesn't Know Anyone's Name — Write WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES
ideas.description: App.jsx:1169 falls back to '🎉 NAME shipped it!' for every worker regardless of role, vibe, or personality. Carlos shipping a backend fix and Maya shipping a component redesign get the exact same celebration. Create WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES = { carlos: { 'crushing': '🔌 Carlos nailed the deploy', 'on-fire': '🧯 Carlos patched it under fire', default: '📦 Carlos shipped the logs' }, maya: { ... }, dave: { ... }, sofia: { ... }, luna: { ... } }. Look up by worker.id and teamVibe.key, fall back to default. Thirty lines of copy that make every completion feel earned. The system for it already exists — just lacks the content.

ideas.title: VIBE_RANK Defined Twice — Identical Map at Module Scope and Inside useEffect
ideas.description: App.jsx:397 defines `const VIBE_RANK = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` at module scope. App.jsx:1010 defines `const vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` inside a useEffect body — identical values, different name. Delete vibeRanking, reference VIBE_RANK. One source of truth. If someone updates one and misses the other, the confetti-on-vibe-upgrade logic silently diverges from the sparkline ranking.

ideas.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS Still Allocated Inside idleWorkers.map()
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1571 declares `const WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS = { carlos: [...], maya: [...], ... }` inside the idleWorkers.map() callback. On every render that has idle workers, a fresh object with five arrays is created and immediately thrown away. This was flagged in review 82 — the content became character-specific (fixed!) but the location never moved. Hoist the constant to module scope beside PING_REACTIONS and WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS. Zero functional change, zero allocation cost per render.

ideas.title: VIBE_WEATHER Widget Shows the Same Emoji Twice
ideas.description: Office.jsx:108-124 defines VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS with emoji baked into the string ('☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength') and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS as a separate map with the same emoji ('☀️'). The render at line 1638-1639 shows both: an icon span with the emoji AND a label span that also leads with the emoji. Users see '☀️ ☀️ Clear skies…'. Fix: strip the leading emoji from VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS strings (the icon span covers it) OR remove the icon span and let the label carry both. Either approach is a one-line edit per entry.

ideas.title: WallClock Vibe Comments Only Visible in SVG title — Touch Devices Get Nothing
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1102 puts the CLOCK_COMMENTS text inside an SVG <title> element. On desktop, mouse hover triggers a browser tooltip. On mobile and touch devices, SVG title is never surfaced. Any user on a phone sees a clock that changes color but gets zero commentary on why Dave is still at his desk at midnight. Fix: render the active clock comment as a small visible text element or tooltip below the clock on tap. The constant is already hoisted and the comment for the current vibe already selected — just needs a visible outlet beyond SVG title.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS Vibe Modulation — Characters Should Sound Different Under Pressure
ideas.description: PING_REACTIONS now has five great character-specific strings per worker. But Carlos responds the same way to a ping during 'slow-day' and 'on-fire'. Sofia's edge-case energy makes sense during 'crushing' but reads odd on a slow afternoon. Add an optional vibe key per worker: { carlos: { 'on-fire': ['The logs are SCREAMING. Reading now.', ...], default: [...] } }. Look up by vibeKey first, fall back to default. No need to cover all five vibes for all five workers — even one or two hot-vibe variants per character adds enormous texture.

ideas.title: Quote Counter Completion Is Silent — Celebrate Finding All 8
ideas.description: App.jsx now correctly caps quoteBonus at BONUS_TAGLINES.length and hides the counter once all 8 taglines are found — which is a real improvement over the old infinite loop. But the transition from '7/8' to no counter is silent. The user found every hidden tagline with no acknowledgment. Add a one-time micro-celebration: when quoteBonus first reaches BONUS_TAGLINES.length, fire a small confetti burst and briefly flash a '✨ all found' badge before the counter fades. The infrastructure (confetti, toast) already exists — this is a single condition and a setCompletionToast call.

ideas.title: Conference Table After-Hours Label Is Inconsistent With Door
ideas.description: Office.jsx:964 sets the ambient label for empty conference table to plain 'locked up' when vibe is after-hours. The door uses DOOR_LABELS['after-hours'] = '🔒 Locked up'. Two adjacent UI elements describe the same state with different text and different emoji treatment. Unify: either match the door's '🔒 Locked up' or use the existing theme '🌙 the table sleeps' — both are better than the current plain-text orphan. One string change.

ideas.title: PING_REACTIONS Vibe Matrix — An Ensemble Where Every Character Has an Arc
ideas.description: Spec: restructure PING_REACTIONS from { workerId: string[] } to { workerId: { vibeKey: string[], default: string[] } }. Sofia during on-fire: 'Incident mode. Writing regression tests NOW. Do not ship anything.' Sofia during slow-day: 'Found a bug. No rush. It has been there for months.' Carlos during crushing: 'The logs are clean for once. I am suspicious.' Carlos during after-hours: 'Why are you pinging me. The servers are fine. I checked.' Dave during on-fire: 'Put on the incident playlist. Already done.' Luna during slow-day: 'I have seventeen concepts. None of them are ready. Same as always.' Each combination is only 2-3 strings deep. Total new copy: ~60 strings. Result: pinging someone becomes an act of discovery — you learn about them AND the current vibe at the same time.

ideas.title: Bloberto Mood Ring — Team Health in a Single Ambient Signal
ideas.description: Bloberto has a face, speech bubble, syncing indicator, and title badge but no continuous real-time ambient signal. Propose a soft animated ring/halo around Bloberto whose color matches VIBE_COLORS, pulse speed maps to active worker ratio (fast = all hands, slow = team idle), and glow intensity scales with vibe rank (VIBE_RANK value 0-4). During on-fire with high activity: fast red pulse. During after-hours with one worker: slow indigo glow. During crushing at full team: fast bright purple. No new data fetches — all signals available from existing props. Carlos wires the threshold values from activeWorkers; maya handles the CSS keyframe spec. Luna specs the mapping table. One element that tells the whole team story at a glance from across the room.
